topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The scent of old paper and faint vanilla hung heavy in the air of the dorm room. I was pacing, a restless energy thrumming just beneath my skin. Every time I glanced toward Liam’s side of the room, my gaze snagged on the open journal lying on his desk. My heart gave a sudden, frantic thump against my ribs. I stopped mid-stride, my breath catching in my throat. He was looking up, a slight furrow in his brow, and a quick, almost imperceptible shift in his posture told me he’d noticed my sudden halt. I swallowed hard, my mouth suddenly dry. I had to know what he was reading.

[story 2]

A prickling sensation ran down my arms as I walked into the room. I couldn't sit still; my feet kept tapping a rapid rhythm against the worn carpet. When I saw Liam, he was engrossed, his head bowed over a spiral-bound book. The way his fingers turned the pages, slow and deliberate, made my stomach clench with a sharp, impatient feeling. I watched, transfixed, as he traced a line of script, his expression unreadable. A small, involuntary sound escaped my lips—a soft, questioning hum. He looked up instantly, his eyes meeting mine, and for a split second, I thought I saw a flicker of something akin to guilt before he quickly masked it with a casual shrug.

[story 3]

I couldn't focus on my calculus notes. My mind was a whirlwind, pulling at every available thread. I kept glancing over at Liam, who was hunched over his desk, bathed in the soft glow of his lamp. There was a certain tension in the air, a silent current of anticipation that made my palms sweat. When I finally dared to approach, my footsteps seemed loud in the quiet room. I paused at the doorway, forcing myself to keep my voice level. "What are you reading?" I asked, the sound of my own voice surprisingly high. Liam closed the book with a soft snap, his eyes wide, and he didn't look away. The sudden stillness felt charged, demanding an answer I wasn't quite ready to give.
